skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating or updating Codex skills with clear triggers, lean instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging workflows.
skill.summary: Create effective Codex skills with structured guidance, reusable resources, and validation steps.
skill.author: BenderFendor

security_audit.summary: Manual review dismisses the critical heuristic and most high-severity static findings as false positives from license text, Markdown examples, and safe validation code. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because its helper scripts write files, set executable permissions, and package all files under a selected skill directory.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Files
medium_findings.description: The packaging script walks every file under the selected skill directory and writes it into a .skill archive. This is expected behavior for a packager, but it can disclose secrets or private files if a user points it at an unclean directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code uses rglob over the full skill directory and zipf.write for every file. The behavior is legitimate, but the inclusion boundary depends on the selected directory contents.

medium_findings.title: Skill Initializer Writes Files and Sets Executable Permissions
medium_findings.description: The initializer creates a new directory tree, writes template files, and marks an example script executable. This supports the skill creation workflow, but it modifies the filesystem at a user-supplied output path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and chmod calls are direct and easy to verify. Risk is moderate because the path is user supplied and no destructive deletion or hidden execution was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer High-Severity Patterns Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are Apache License links, weak-crypto matches are ordinary words in documentation or license text, and many backtick findings are Markdown code formatting. No network calls, command execution APIs, credential reads, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed these findings are documentation text, license boilerplate, safe YAML parsing, or code examples. Searches found no prompt injection markers or external network code.

low_findings.title: Validator Reads Local Skill Content Only
low_findings.description: The validator reads SKILL.md and parses YAML frontmatter with yaml.safe_load. This is local validation behavior and does not execute skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code reads one local Markdown file and uses safe_load. No dynamic execution path was found around validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The packaging script walks every file under the selected skill directory and writes it into a .skill archive. This is expected behavior for a packager, but it can disclose secrets or private files if a user points it at an unclean directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code uses rglob over the full skill directory and zipf.write for every file. The behavior is legitimate, but the inclusion boundary depends on the selected directory contents.

dangerous_patterns.title: Skill Initializer Writes Files and Sets Executable Permissions
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer creates a new directory tree, writes template files, and marks an example script executable. This supports the skill creation workflow, but it modifies the filesystem at a user-supplied output path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and chmod calls are direct and easy to verify. Risk is moderate because the path is user supplied and no destructive deletion or hidden execution was found.

content.value_statement: Building reusable AI skills can be inconsistent without clear structure and validation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through skill design, scaffolding, resource planning, and packaging.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to design a skill with clear triggers and concise instructions.
Guides when to add scripts, references, or assets to a skill folder.
Provides a Python initializer for generating a starter skill structure.
Provides validation guidance for SKILL.md frontmatter and naming rules.
Provides packaging guidance for creating distributable .skill archives.
Describes iteration steps for improving skills after real usage.
limitations: It does not replace human review of generated skill instructions.
It can package unintended files if the skill folder contains private material.
It focuses on Codex-style skills and may need adaptation for other systems.
It provides starter templates, not complete domain-specific skill content.

use_cases.title: Create a New Automation Skill
use_cases.description: Design a focused skill with triggers, workflow steps, and reusable helper resources.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review an existing skill for clarity, resource placement, validation, and packaging readiness.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Skill for Distribution
use_cases.description: Validate structure and package a completed skill into a shareable .skill archive.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Codex skill for [task]. Ask only the essential questions, then draft the SKILL.md structure.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Skill Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these example requests for [domain]. Recommend which scripts, references, and assets the skill should include.

prompt_templates.title: Update an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing skill for trigger clarity, concise instructions, useful resources, and validation gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Package and Validate a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare this skill for distribution. Validate the structure, identify risky bundled files, and package it only after issues are resolved.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for rotating and splitting PDF files.
output: A focused skill plan with a clear trigger description.
A recommended scripts folder for deterministic PDF operations.
Validation steps before the skill is packaged.

output_examples.input: Improve this database helper skill before publishing it.
output: Recommendations for moving long schema details into references.
A packaging checklist that excludes private files.

output_examples.input: Package this completed skill for a teammate.
output: Validation results for naming and frontmatter.
A warning to inspect included files before archiving.
A shareable skill package after validation passes.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed reference material into linked files.
Test bundled scripts before relying on them in a skill workflow.
Inspect the skill folder for secrets or unrelated files before packaging.
anti_patterns: Do not include broad documentation files that do not support the skill workflow.
Do not hide important trigger rules deep in the body of SKILL.md.
Do not package a working directory that contains private files or temporary artifacts.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create or update Codex skill folders with SKILL.md instructions and optional reusable resources.

faq.question: Does it generate complete domain expertise automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure and workflow guidance. Domain content still needs accurate source material.

faq.question: Can it include scripts in a skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when scripts are useful and includes helper scripts for initialization, validation, and packaging.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning. Review packaged folders carefully to avoid including private files.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I check before packaging?
faq.answer: Validate SKILL.md, test bundled scripts, and remove secrets, temporary files, and unrelated assets.
